check.description: Run the repo's canonical integrity gate (make check). Use to verify the tree is green before review/commit.
check.template:
Run the repo's canonical integrity gate exactly as CI does:

    make check

Report the result. If it fails, do NOT commit or push — read the diagnostic, fix the underlying issue, and re-run until it exits clean. This is the same set .github/workflows/integrity.yml enforces, so `make check` staying green means local and CI agree.

fix.description: Apply the repo's offline fixers then re-verify (make fix). Use when make check reports drift that has an apply-mode fixer.
fix.template:
Run the repo's apply-mode fixers in dependency order, then re-verify:

    make fix

`make fix` runs reconcile-counts → backfill-evidence → tier-stack → sync-plugin-docs, then re-runs `make check`, so a clean exit means the tree is actually green. Report which fixers applied and the final `make check` status.

sync.description: Re-sync plugin/docs/ from the authoritative root docs (./sync-plugin-docs.sh). Use after editing CATALOG.md/WORKFLOW.md/STACK.md/evaluations.
sync.template:
Re-sync the derived plugin docs copy from the authoritative root docs:

    ./sync-plugin-docs.sh

Then run `./sync-plugin-docs.sh --check` to confirm the tree no longer reports sync drift. Report the result. Note: root docs are authoritative; plugin/docs/ is a synced copy — never edit plugin/docs/ directly.
